Implement schema-aware semantic merge for refs/prism/** so shared-coordination conflicts are resolved by payload semantics and conflict classification instead of generic Git text merges or blind CAS retry loops.

Introduce explicit schema_version envelopes and compatibility validation for every authoritative shared-coordination payload, including the summary manifest, runtime descriptors, and the future task and claim shard payload shapes.

Split runtime liveness and discovery out of the repo-wide live summary onto per-runtime authoritative refs, and keep the summary runtime index as a derived aggregate instead of the hot heartbeat write path.

Expose diagnostics and operator signals for schema compatibility, summary freshness, per-ref-family CAS retry rates, shard contention, runtime lag, compaction health, and archive state so operators can see when the 1000-agent topology is behaving badly.
Rework lease validity so task and claim records carry durable lease-boundary facts while runtime liveness comes from runtime refs, making lease freshness a join instead of a global summary rewrite.

Move authoritative task and claim mutations off the single live summary ref onto fixed hash-sharded task and claim ref families with deterministic shard routing and compare-and-swap publication per shard.

Audit the current shared-coordination implementation against the v1 architecture and freeze the authoritative file/ref map for summary, runtime, task, claim, read-model, startup-checkpoint, and MCP surfaces.

Add bounded hot-state pruning and an archive plane for summary, runtime, task, and claim data, including continuity metadata and archive-aware read behavior so active coordination scales with current work instead of repository age.

Refactor the read path and materializer so the repo-wide summary ref is a compact derived read model with explicit freshness visibility and authoritative fallback reads from shard and runtime refs when needed.

Build migration, repair, and backfill tooling that can lift the current single-live-summary topology into versioned runtime, task, and claim shard refs without losing authoritative coordination continuity.

Cut over the remaining docs and validation suites, and add end-to-end coverage for mixed-version readers, shard routing, archive continuity, semantic merge conflicts, and high-contention shared-coordination behavior.
